title: Fix podman hangs when detecting startup error in container attached mode
body:
Signed-off-by: Marco Vedovati <mvedovati@suse.com>

The initial resize command sent to the terminal window over the resize
channel may never be delivered in case of error.

Hence it is necessary to consume all data from the resize channel to
avoid a deadlock on startup.

comments.body: I think it is also worth to refactor `attachCtr` `startAttachCtr` to handle the tty attach with a common function to avoid code duplication.

> I think it is also worth to refactor `attachCtr` `startAttachCtr` to handle the tty attach with a common function to avoid code duplication.

I agree that there looks like a fair bit of duplication between those two functions.  I'm in favor of adding a new helper function, or consolidating them to a single function with a `start` boolean argument, or some such to DRY things up.

For a clogged `resize` channel, I'm having trouble understanding the issue.  There doesn't seem to be a size cap on the channel, so we should be able to write resize events into the channel regardless of whether there's a reader (I think?).  Then if the attach fails, we'll die with some events still in the channel, and the channel and dangling events will be garbage-collected once the last reference to them is gone.  I don't see why we need to drain the channel manually.  Can you provide a bit more detail about who's deadlocking over those dangling events?

Thanks for your comment, as it allowed me to dig a little bit more into the problem and sorry if my initial explanation was not detailed enough

The root cause is a deadlock between two channel writes made by two different goroutines:
1. `resizeTty() : go func(){} : sendUpdate()` is sending a resize message thru `resize` right at the beginning, but the channel is never read if some startup error occurs.
2. Upon program termination, `startAttachCtr() : defer func(){} ` is telling the goroutine in "1." to stop via the `resizeTerminate` channel. But that guy is still waiting for the write to `resize` to complete so the the termination message is never read.

I think the go deadlock detection does not kick in because not all goroutines are seen as asleep. E.g. `os/signal Notify()` is enough to have the deadlock not detected.

(cc @vrothberg)
Code LGTM. And I definitely wouldn't mind seeing work to refactor the two attach functions together, given the level of similarity between them.
Great, should I open a new PR or updating this one is enough?
I think we should merge this one as-is for the deadlock fix and handle the refactor in a new one, to ensure we get this patch in for the release.
LGTM, tested locally and it works

:pushpin: Commit 0d5e825 has been approved by `giuseppe`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=giuseppe 0d5e825aae118ecb17c506f1bafcdddea3e325e9 -->

:sunny: Test successful - [status-papr](https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/libpod/333ab8c21120c3f7e6b3df7a6a49ca860f079266.1530194636412412160/index.html)
Approved by: giuseppe
Pushing 333ab8c21120c3f7e6b3df7a6a49ca860f079266 to master...

> But that guy is still waiting for the write to resize to complete so the the termination message is never read.

This seems like the real bug to me.  I'll see if I can work up a follow-up to move those writes into a `select` that's also reading from `resizeTerminate`.
